messages.subject: [R-jobs] Job opening for statistician at USDA
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Job opening for statistician at USDA
messages.body_plain:
CENTER FOR VETERINARY BIOLOGICS
UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE

Join our team of statisticians and data scientists in 2022

We bring a broad perspective to our consulting and analytical work with
scientists at the
USDA National Centers for Animal Health. Our Statistics Section is looking
for good communicators who are comfortable dealing with various aspects of
practical scientific issues.

We are looking for skills that support the
 -? Statistical design, analysis, and interpretation of scientific studies
 ?- Application of statistical principles to specific problems in applied
disciplines
 ?- Effective communication with scientists about practical issues

We?d also like to know about your background in areas that may advance our
work, such as laboratory applications, epidemiology, machine learning, or
statistical programming.

Applicants must be United States citizens.  This position *is* remote
eligible.  (The USAJOBS post is a bit confusing on this point.)

How to Apply:  https://www.usajobs.gov/job/665769000
(The deadline to apply is this Friday July 22.)

The Center for Veterinary Biologics regulates vaccines,
immuno-therapeutics, diagnostics, and other biologics used to manage animal
diseases. It is located on the campus of the National Centers for Animal
Health in Ames, Iowa, where it maintains a close affiliation with the other
animal disease centers on the campus and with nearby Iowa State University.
Ames is known for its stimulating cultural environment, pleasant
surroundings, and stable economy. It is 40 minutes north of Des Moines
International Airport.

Employment benefits include flexible work schedule, ten paid federal
holidays, liberal leave allowance (annual leave, sick leave, and family
friendly leave policy), retirement and thrift savings plans, life insurance
program, and health insurance program.

More info contact Amy Lock, Statistics Section Leader, USDA-CVB;
amy.b.lock at usda.gov

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Statistician / R developer
messages.body_plain:
The Statistics Division (ESS) of the Food and Agricultural Organization of the United Nations (FAO) is looking for consultants with a strong background in the R 
statistical programming language. They will contribute to develop methods and routines for the corporate Statistical Working System (SWS), which is an essential tool for 
the improvement and harmonization of FAO statistical processes. The main objectives of the new phase of the SWS project are the incorporation of new statistical domains, 
the capability to manage and integrate multiple data sources, integration with the new Statistical Data Warehouse, development of new Shiny applications to analyse and 
validate data.
 
Responsibilities
 
Consultants will be responsible for the development of methods, standards, and tools with the objective to improve the efficiency of FAO's statistical system and the 
quality of its output. In particular, they will:
 
- Develop, test, and implement optimum imputation, analysis, validation, or other statistical methods for teams that operate in various statistical domains (agriculture, 
trade, environment, fisheries, forestry, food security).
- Provide high-level recommendations on methods and their implementation
- Design and develop R modules architecture to implement the methods and deliver script in accordance
- Document the statistical methods and corresponding R scripts
- Develop and maintain Shiny applications
- Any other duty as required
 
 
Core skills
 
- Dynamic statistician equipped with both highly advanced statistical and R-programming skills
- Ability to quickly master and apply new methods
- Ability to make recommendations and advise decision-makers on highly technical issues
- General understanding of databases and complex technological systems
- Ability to understand statistical user requirements
- Ability to write modular code
- Proven ability to self-start, progress quickly up a steep learning curve, assume initiative and to multi-task
- Focus on delivery and on meeting tight deadlines
- Knowledge of agricultural statistics and/or food commodities would be an advantage, though not required
- Effective collaborator, in particular familiar with git and GitHub
 
Minimum requirements
 
- Postgraduate in statistics or other relevant quantitative field
- At least 3 years of experience in the use of R
- Mastering of data.table and tidyverse packages
- Good knowledge of Shiny
- Language skills (working language is English, knowledge of additional UN official languages is an advantage)
- Good writing skills

Send your CV before July 31st to christian.mongeau [AT] fao.org (replace [AT] with @). We will consider also late applications for future positions.
